name: ExtensionImagingSelection_Endpoint
title: R5: The network service providing retrieval for the images referenced in the imaging selection (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ImagingSelection.endpoint` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ImagingSelection.endpoint` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Endpoint)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `ImagingSelection.endpoint` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Endpoint)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ImagingSelection.endpoint` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.endpoint` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The network service providing retrieval for the images referenced in the imaging selection (new)
element.comment:
Element `ImagingSelection.endpoint` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ImagingSelection` is not mapped.
Element `ImagingSelection.endpoint` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Typical endpoint types include DICOM WADO-RS, which is used to retrieve DICOM instances in native or rendered (e.g., JPG, PNG) formats using a RESTful API; DICOM WADO-URI, which can similarly retrieve native or rendered instances, except using an HTTP query-based approach.

element.short: The network service providing retrieval for the images referenced in the imaging selection
element.definition: The network service providing retrieval access to the selected images, frames, etc. See implementation notes for information about using DICOM endpoints.
element.comment: Typical endpoint types include DICOM WADO-RS, which is used to retrieve DICOM instances in native or rendered (e.g., JPG, PNG) formats using a RESTful API; DICOM WADO-URI, which can similarly retrieve native or rendered instances, except using an HTTP query-based approach.
